servinfo.title: Stall Test
servinfo.ptxt: This test is to check overall performance of A/T and engine by measuring stall speed in “D” and “R” ranges. Be sure to perform this test only when ATF is at normal operating temperature and its level is between FULL and LOW marks.

item.ptxt: Do not run engine at stall for more than 5 seconds continuously, or fluid temperature may rise excessively.
After performing stall test, be sure to leave engine running at idle for longer than 1 minute before another stall test.

s1.ptxt: Apply parking brake and block wheels.
Connect SUZUKI scan tool to DLC (1).
Display “Data list” mode on scan tool.
Start engine with select lever in “P” range.
Shift select lever to “D” range and depress accelerator pedal fully while watching tachometer. Read engine rpm quickly when it has become constant (stall speed).
Release accelerator pedal immediately after stall speed is checked.
In the same way, check stall speed in “R” range.
Stall speed should be within the following specification.

condition.ptxt: Lower than standard level in both “D” and “R” ranges

action.ptxt: Replace torque converter referring to A/T Assembly Reassembly .

condition.ptxt: Higher than standard level in “D” range

ps.ptxt: Malfunction of pressure control solenoid valve (Low line pressure)

action.ptxt: Valve Body Assembly Removal and Installation .
Forward and Reverse Clutch Assembly Preliminary Check .
Check one-way clutch No.2 referring to A/T Assembly Disassembly .

condition.ptxt: Higher than standard level in “R” range

ps.ptxt: Malfunction of pressure control solenoid valve (Low line pressure)

action.ptxt: Valve Body Assembly Removal and Installation .
Forward and Reverse Clutch Assembly Preliminary Check .
Check 1st and reverse brake referring to A/T Assembly Reassembly .

condition.ptxt: Higher than standard level in both “D” and “R” ranges

ps.ptxt: Malfunction of pressure control solenoid valve (Low line pressure)

action.ptxt: Valve Body Assembly Removal and Installation .
A/T Oil Strainer Assembly Removal and Installation .
